title: tenant.plan-changed payload (v1)
description: Emitted when a tenant's plan actually changes. Producer: tenant-service — published from THREE call sites (apps/tenant-service/src/routes/tenants.ts stripe-status flip, and apps/tenant-service/src/routes/plan-changes.ts on manual approve + on stripe-webhook activate). The payloads DIVERGE across call sites, so only tenantId/fromPlan/toPlan are always present: the stripe-flip path sends `source`+`stripeStatus` (no changedByUserId/requestId); the approve/activate paths send `changedByUserId`+`requestId` (and `source:"stripe"` on activate). All the variant fields are therefore OPTIONAL here. Note the subject leaf is domain=tenant, event=plan-changed (subject tenant.{tenantId}.tenant.plan-changed).
x-producer: tenant-service (stripe-status flip route, plan-change approve, plan-change stripe activate)
x-consumers: billing-service (sync TenantPlanCache + auto-disable agents the new plan no longer allows)
notification-service (PLAN_CHANGE_APPROVED to the tenant + super-admins; source flag distinguishes stripe vs manual)

changedByUserId.description: Reviewer/actor who changed the plan. Present on the approve/activate paths (activate uses "stripe-webhook" when no reviewer). ABSENT on the stripe-status-flip route.

requestId.description: Originating PlanChangeRequest id. Present on the approve/activate paths; ABSENT on the stripe-status-flip route.

source.description: "stripe" when the change came from a Stripe payment/flip; omitted (treated as "manual") otherwise. Consumers key stripe-vs-manual copy off this.

stripeStatus.description: Stripe subscription status. Present only on the stripe-status-flip route.
